rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE J. MALINOWSKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Patricia Hand Amaro failed to follow Probate Code 9252 and instead paid her own creditor claim without court authority. 2) Patricia Hand Amaro has not filed an allowance/rejection of the $113.61 creditor claim. Patricia Hand Amaro has not filed proof of satisfaction of the $113.61 creditor claim. 3) Patricia Hand Amaro failed to follow Probate Code 9831 and did not receive court confirmation to settle alleged claims. Patricia Hand originally characterized these payments as preliminary distributions when questioned now alleges they are settlement payments made without required court approval. 4) Patricia Hand Amaro used estate funds to provide her current counsel with a retainer. Does not explain how much of a retainer was given to the attorney. 5) Court to determine how to proceed on the disclaimers.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN RUTH LANE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Determination Of Entitlement Of Estate Distribution (Probate Code Section 11700)
rulings.Rulings: continued to 8/10/21 (first available date). the POS in the efiling queue shows notice mailed 6/29/21 for hearing on 7/12/21, which is less than the 15 days required. new notice must be mailed with the continued hearing date. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: continued to 8/3/21. the POS of the NOH was uploaded along with the amended petition, and showed notice mailed 6/25/21, the date the pleadings were uploaded. this transaction is a perfect example of why a POS should not be filed contemporaneously with the pleading requiring notice, since the amended petiiton was not actually filed until july 1, 2021, so notice mailed 6/25/21 was before the pleading was filed and is therefore invalid under local rules. new notice is required.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/24/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies were received by the Examiner by 6/30/21, as noted in the tentative ruling posted for the hearing on 6/2/21; however, in hopes of not continuing the hearing again, the E-filed papers were reviewed online as a one-time courtesy. The "Declaration by Petitioner" filed on 6/25/21 AMENDED the accounting, extending the accounting period and showing a greater amount of disbursements. An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] The "Declaration by Petitioner" did not identify the other interested parties: the heirs or beneficiaries of Eleanor Zelman and their addresses must be set forth. Certified Letters (or equivalent thereof) certified within 60 days must be filed. [LRSF 14.36 G.] The copy of the Letters attached to the "Declaration by Petitioner" was dated 5/10/18. The bank statements filed on 6/25/21 end on 5/28/21; the accounting was extended to 6/15/21. The financial statements must encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting period. Some of the statements, furthermore, reflect checks that were not included in the disbursements in the accounting, including $300 on 2/8/21 and $1,900 on 5/3/21. If there was $154,995.72 in cash on hand as of 6/15/21, after subtracting the statutory fee/commission, there should be $143,539.98 on hand to be distributed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed on or after 6/25/21 must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/9/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PETER DAVID ANDRADE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/24/21. (No earlier availability.) The only I&A filed in this proceeding, on 12/1/20, showed assets totaling $1,381,317.79, not $1,331,317.79. The "Supplement" filed o n 6/7/21 AMENDED the accounting as well as some of the relief requested in the petition. An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a). The new accounting does not balance. Notice must be made directly to an interested party, unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of a second person. [CRC 7.51(a)(3).] The address for Patricia Joyce Welch is different from her address at the outset of these proceedings and is now apparently the same as Nancy Oehler's address. A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.a.] The written consents of the heirs to the distribution of the tangible personal property must also be filed. Receipts of principal, such as "prorated holdover rent" and "holdover property taxes" attributable to Patricia Welch or for refunds of payments made out of the estate, are not includable in the fee base. Costs advanced by the administrator and reimbursed to him are missing some descriptions or original payees (e.g., E-filing fee with any description of what was E-filed and no payees identified for lump sum meals). The filing fee for the initial petition was $450, not $590. There was no need for the estate to incur CourtCall costs when appearances were not required and Zoom appearances have been available since March 2020. The Probate Referee commission was $1,338.47, not $1,338.06. Was it really necessary to develop photos of the SF property? (The administrator alleged in paragraph 23 of the petition that the estate did not own any real property The creditor's claim was filed on 11/30/20, not 11/3/20.) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution," or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust]." [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Courtesy copies and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/10/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLAUDE L HEATER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/11/2021. 1) The Notice of Hearing filed 6/28/2021 does not state on page 2 the date of mailing and the date signed under penalty of perjury. Matter continued again to file a DE-120 with proper notice. 2) See LRSF 14.35 D. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. 3) Please provide the legal basis for keeping all cash assets in a single Wells Fargo account when the FDIC limit is $250,000.00. 4) Please provide a legal basis for the personal property not being listed on attachment 2 on the I&A not being appraised by the referee. 5) Please provide a legal basis for the alleged creditor claim being paid by the estate when it appears this should have been brought as an 850 petition by the trust. Please provide the legal basis for the distribution of these estate assets. Please explain how this distribution reduces the remaining assets of the estate. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/26/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YAMILET DEL CARMEN GUTIERREZ-GONZALEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/10/2021. 1) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 2) Petitioner is not listed on page 4 under #8. Please provide the name, relationship to decedent, age, and address of Petitioner. 3) Please provide a legal basis for whether guardian ad litem needs to be appointed for the minor heirs. 4) Please provide a legal basis for the request for $10,000.00 in bond. It appears full bond should be required. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/26/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
